title: Politics6 hours agoThank the Supreme Court for Trump's Latest Attack on the Federal ReserveThe justices gave him a loaded weapon
raw_response: {"rating": -7.0, "lean": "Left", "explanation": "This is an opinion/editorial piece that presents a sustained, strongly left-leaning argument casting the Supreme Court's conservative majority and President Trump as collaborators in an authoritarian power grab. The author's prose is saturated with anti-Trump and anti-conservative-Court language, with no countervailing perspective or balance offered. The intensity and consistency of ideological framing warrants a strong Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline and subhead — 'Thank the Supreme Court for Trump's Latest Attack on the Federal Reserve' and 'The justices gave him a loaded weapon' — frame the Court's conservative majority as culpable enablers of Trump's alleged authoritarianism, entirely in the author's own editorial voice. (2) The author repeatedly characterizes the conservative justices with dismissive language: 'foolishly thought,' 'hubris,' 'silly Trump's allies in robes,' 'rushed breathlessly forward' — all pejorative judgments in the author's own prose. (3) Trump is described as a 'would-be authoritarian,' a 'wannabe king,' and his actions are characterized as a 'constitutional coup' — language that frames the subject not as a political actor but as a threat to democracy, a hallmark of left-leaning framing. (4) The author describes AG Jeanine Pirro as Trump's 'longtime toady,' a strongly pejorative characterization in their own voice. (5) The unitary executive theory is framed entirely as dangerous and authoritarian, compared to democratic erosion in Turkey and Hungary, with no acknowledgment of its mainstream conservative legal pedigree or any counterarguments in its favor. (6) The article frames the DOJ investigation of Powell as self-evidently a 'sham prosecution' and 'bogus' — the author's own conclusions presented as fact without qualifying language. (7) The piece draws a direct line from Court rulings to autocracy, reflecting a belief in the need for government independence from executive control and skepticism of deregulation — alignments with left-leaning values regarding checks on executive power and institutional independence. (8) No conservative or pro-administration perspective is included or even acknowledged as a legitimate viewpoint anywhere in the piece."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is an opinion/editorial piece that presents a sustained, strongly left-leaning argument casting the Supreme Court's conservative majority and President Trump as collaborators in an authoritarian power grab. The author's prose is saturated with anti-Trump and anti-conservative-Court language, with no countervailing perspective or balance offered. The intensity and consistency of ideological framing warrants a strong Left rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The headline and subhead — 'Thank the Supreme Court for Trump's Latest Attack on the Federal Reserve' and 'The justices gave him a loaded weapon' — frame the Court's conservative majority as culpable enablers of Trump's alleged authoritarianism, entirely in the author's own editorial voice. (2) The author repeatedly characterizes the conservative justices with dismissive language: 'foolishly thought,' 'hubris,' 'silly Trump's allies in robes,' 'rushed breathlessly forward' — all pejorative judgments in the author's own prose. (3) Trump is described as a 'would-be authoritarian,' a 'wannabe king,' and his actions are characterized as a 'constitutional coup' — language that frames the subject not as a political actor but as a threat to democracy, a hallmark of left-leaning framing. (4) The author describes AG Jeanine Pirro as Trump's 'longtime toady,' a strongly pejorative characterization in their own voice. (5) The unitary executive theory is framed entirely as dangerous and authoritarian, compared to democratic erosion in Turkey and Hungary, with no acknowledgment of its mainstream conservative legal pedigree or any counterarguments in its favor. (6) The article frames the DOJ investigation of Powell as self-evidently a 'sham prosecution' and 'bogus' — the author's own conclusions presented as fact without qualifying language. (7) The piece draws a direct line from Court rulings to autocracy, reflecting a belief in the need for government independence from executive control and skepticism of deregulation — alignments with left-leaning values regarding checks on executive power and institutional independence. (8) No conservative or pro-administration perspective is included or even acknowledged as a legitimate viewpoint anywhere in the piece.
